label: Rate Limiter
description: Fixed-window throttle for fan-out to rate-limited APIs. Per-(tenant, key) counter; choose block (sleep until reset), drop (return without invoking), or error (throw) on overflow.

settings.name: key
settings.label: Bucket key
settings.description: Namespaced counter key. Buckets are isolated by (tenant, key) so two tenants can use the same key without sharing a window.

settings.name: maxCalls
settings.label: Max calls per window
settings.description: Upper bound on calls within a single window. Counter resets at window boundary.

settings.name: windowSeconds
settings.label: Window (seconds)
settings.description: Length of the fixed window. Defaults to 60s; 1s windows behave like a per-second limit.

settings.name: onLimit
settings.label: On limit
settings.description: block = wait until next window opens (counts as allowed afterwards); drop = return without invoking downstream (allowed=false); error = throw a runtime error.

settings.name: scope
settings.label: Scope
settings.description: Where the counter lives. V1 supports `local` (in-memory per-process, accurate on a single replica). V1.1 will add `redis` for multi-replica deployments.

ai.shortDescription: Throttle fan-out to rate-limited APIs with a fixed-window counter.
ai.whenToUse: Place upstream of an http_request/agent_single/mcp_tool node that calls a third-party API with a published rate limit. Same bucket key across a loop body shares the window.
ai.examplePrompt: limit to 5 calls per second on the bucket 'api:openai', block if exceeded
ai.promptHints: Output: {{steps.X.allowed}} (true = downstream should proceed; false on drop). For multi-tenant flows, keep the key generic — the executor adds the tenant scope automatically.

outputAssertions.name: has_envelope
outputAssertions.errorMessage: rate_limiter output is missing the {allowed, key} envelope.
